observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed –

a.	continuously abide and ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of COA, DBM, DILG, GC for GOCCs, DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 regarding entitlement, release, use, reporting and audit of confidential and/or intelligence funds in providing services that it may serve in protecting the safety of its constituents; and

b.	communicate with the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Unit (ICFAU) in writing to inquire about the status of the liquidation reports submitted to them for the unliquidated cash advances of the Municipal Mayor in the years 2012, 2013, and 2015 totaling ₱600,000.00 and request for the issuance of the corresponding Credit Notices for the recording of the liquidation in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: The Audit Team awaits the submission of a copy of the letter to ICFAU requesting Credit Notices

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed that –

a.	the end-users diligently study and prepare the project proposals, in order to deliver the benefits intended to the identified beneficiaries; and 

b.	return all unutilized funds to the source agencies as stipulated in the agreements.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Office adequately and properly plan the timetable of implementation of the projects to ensure the timely and full implementation of the planned projects and to prevent the delayed implementation of projects in the future.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II. Observation No. 5 with modification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the –

a.	Management require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to fully comply with its mandated functions as enumerated in R.A. No. 9003 and its IRR; and

b.	Local Chief Executive, through the MSWMB –

•	revisit and modify priorities, if applicable, as the plan is being implemented; and

•	pursue its plan, as contained in its MSWMP, to establish a long-term institutionalized process aimed at having law abiding citizens through the implementation of laws, rules and regulation and develop on enormous number of people who have the love and care for the environment.
observations.managementAction:
The MENRO had started the updating of the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan. 


The MENRO had distributed ordinances and IECs to all barangays.
observations.reason: The Management is currently updating the Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP as of the report date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Municipal Accounting Office–

a.	perform a review on all transactions relating to business taxes which led to the erroneous calculated tax due resulting to under collection totaling ₱194,825.70 and submit the result in writing to the Audit Team for further evaluation and audit decision, without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of Charge, if warranted by evidence;

b.	revisit the tax assessment and tax collection made on the provided data that lacks sufficient and appropriate details and submit the result in writing to the Audit Team; and  

c.	 effect appropriate accounting treatment on the overcollections of ₱631,904.52 based on existing standards.
observations.managementAction:
The MTO had contacted the taxpayers thru phone in settling underpayments, while those who did not respond yet will be notified thru demand letter.





MTO will do the necessary adjustments for the overpayments upon renewal of their business permits.
observations.reason:
The under-collection has a remaining balance of ₱17,979.00 still to be collected.





The over-collected amount of ₱137,144.91 is yet to be applied in the next collection cycle.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended that the Management (1) develop and implement a
standardized methodology for measuring impairment allowances to ensure the
proper recognition of allowances for impaired receivables; and (2) direct the
Municipal Accountant to disclose information on the measurement of impairment.

2.5 We recommended that the MTO review and align the bonding limits with
the intended purpose of cash advances.

3.12 We recommended that the Management –
a. establish a written policy of the useful lives of infrastructure assets,
based on the experience of the entity which may consider industry
historical data and condition assessments, environmental and usage
factors, benchmarks or guidance, and engineering estimates, where
applicable; and
b. direct the Municipal Accountant to:
(1) recognize the depreciation of infrastructure assets, if the
estimates provided by the in-house engineer are warranted,
Accounts Debit Credit
Prior Period Adjustment ₱110,275,899.31
Accumulated Depreciation ₱110,275,899.31
; and
(2) disclose information related to the useful lives of the
infrastructure assets.

4.8 We recommended that the GSO seek external assistance from the
appropriate government agencies to recover needed documents to facilitate the
renewal of the registration and insurance of the five motor vehicles.

5.9 We recommended that the a) LPRAT initiate a follow-up with the NAPC on
the approval of the proposed amendments to the Integrated Community Food
Production program; and b) OMA cause the return the remaining balance of
₱165,288.30 to the DOT in accordance with the terms of the MOA.

6.9 Given the transitory period, we recommended that the –
a. MBO (1) plan the full funding of the leave credits; and (2) realign the OPB-
Vacation and Sick Leave to TLB for consistency of reports;
b. OMA (1) accrue earned leaves using appropriate accounts; and (2) disclose
information related to employee leave benefits; and
c. HRMO provide the documentation on the measurement of accumulated
leave credits to the OMA.

7.7 We recommended that the MTO –
a. implement a verification process requiring a secondary review and validation
of all business tax assessments by qualified personnel, effective until the
eLGU is fully implemented;
b. collect the remaining under-collection of ₱17,979.00 for CY 2023 and
₱117,376.65 for CY 2024; and
c. apply the over-collections of ₱137,144.91 for CY 2023 and ₱9,131.50 for CY
2024.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Municipal General Services Office (MGSO), to conduct periodic physical inventory count of supplies, prepare, and submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively, in compliance with the set guidelines.

We recommended and Management agreed that –

a.	instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count on the Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) for all funds, whether acquired through purchase or donation, including those constructed by administration and found at station on the succeeding years to establish the correct balances reported in the financial statement at year end; and

b.	establish controls in monitoring and maintenance of government vehicles records in improving the timely and complete registration and availment of insurance under LTO and GSIS, respectively.

We recommended and Management agreed that the –

a.	Municipal Accountant –

•	record all the land holdings of the Municipal Government using the assessed value, if the acquisition cost could not be established, for a fair and balance presentation of the financial statements;

•	effect the necessary adjustment for the recorded lot in the accounting records with no corresponding land title; and

•	ensure that all the real properties booked up in the accounting records are all titled and registered under the name of the Municipal Government; and

b.	Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal General Services Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor –

•	maintain the required RPLC and PC on all real properties comprising the Land account; and

•	provide complete information on all real properties in the Land account to facilitate verification and reconciliation and effect the necessary adjustments after reconciliation thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed that the – 

a.	BAC strictly –

•	Exercise extra diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities in the procurement activities; and

•	Ensure compliance with the requirements of RA No. 9184 and its IRR and evaluate thoroughly the technical, financial and legal qualification of the prospective bidders before the declaration of winning bidders; and

b.	Municipal Accountant check the completeness of the supporting documents before affixing her signature in the disbursement vouchers and together with the BAC, submit the lacking documents for the evaluation of the Audit Team without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension, if warranted by evidence.

We recommended and Management agreed that the –

a.	Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (MDRRMO) to –

•	submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month for verification and review; and

•	monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF based on the programs, projects and activities listed in the LDRRMIP to ensure the proper implementation of the planned activities;

b.	Municipal General Services Office prepare and submit the mandatory reports for the donated relief goods and donated PPE in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 25, 2014; and

c.	Management comply with Items 2.2.6 to 2.2.8 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 and DILG MC No. 2010-101 dated September 23, 2010.

With the above observations, we recommended and Management agreed to –

a.	continuously comply with the provisions of Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) –Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG)-Department of Budget and Management (DBM)- National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 as guidance in the localization of Magna Carta of Women (MCW) specifically in the preparation of Gender and Development (GAD) Plan and Budget; and

b.	submit to the Audit Team the action plan in addressing the delays and non-implementation of the planned PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council judiciously plan, monitor, evaluate and prioritize the implementation of PPAs to maximize the utilization of the 20% development fund on programs/projects/activities outlined under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and achieve the desired benefits in socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended that – the end-users diligently study and prepare the project proposals, in order to deliver the benefits intended to the identified beneficiaries; and return all unutilized funds to the source agencies as stipulated in the agreements.

1.8.	We recommended and Management agreed that the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Municipal General Services Office (MGSO), to conduct periodic physical inventory count of supplies, prepare, and submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively, in compliance with the set guidelines.

2.4.	We recommended and Management agreed that –
instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count on the Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE) for all funds, whether acquired through purchase or donation, including those constructed by administration and found at station on the succeeding years to establish the correct balances reported in the financial statement at year end; and
establish controls in monitoring and maintenance of government vehicles records in improving the timely and complete registration and availment of insurance under LTO and GSIS, respectively.

3.4.	We recommended and Management agreed that the –
Municipal Accountant –
record all the land holdings of the Municipal Government using the assessed value, if the acquisition cost could not be established, for a fair and balance presentation of the financial statements;
effect the necessary adjustment for the recorded lot in the accounting records with no corresponding land title; and
ensure that all the real properties booked up in the accounting records are all titled and registered under the name of the Municipal Government; and
Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal General Services Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor –
maintain the required RPLC and PC on all real properties comprising the Land account; and
provide complete information on all real properties in the Land account to facilitate verification and reconciliation and effect the necessary adjustments after reconciliation thereof.

4.6.	We recommended and Management agreed –
continuously abide and ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of COA, DBM, DILG, GC for GOCCs, DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 regarding entitlement, release, use, reporting and audit of confidential and/or intelligence funds in providing services that it may serve in protecting the safety of its constituents; and
communicate with the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Unit (ICFAU) in writing to inquire about the status of the liquidation reports submitted to them for the unliquidated cash advances of the Municipal Mayor in the years 2012, 2013, and 2015 totaling ₱600,000.00 and request for the issuance of the corresponding Credit Notices for the recording of the liquidation in the books of accounts.

5.5.	We reiterated prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed that –
the end-users diligently study and prepare the project proposals, in order to deliver the benefits intended to the identified beneficiaries; and
return all unutilized funds to the source agencies as stipulated in the agreements.
5.6.	Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Office adequately and properly plan the timetable of implementation of the projects to ensure the timely and full implementation of the planned projects and to prevent the delayed implementation of projects in the future.

6.8.	We recommended and Management agreed that the –
BAC strictly –
Exercise extra diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities in the procurement activities; and
Ensure compliance with the requirements of RA No. 9184 and its IRR and evaluate thoroughly the technical, financial and legal qualification of the prospective bidders before the declaration of winning bidders; and
Municipal Accountant check the completeness of the supporting documents before affixing her signature in the disbursement vouchers and together with the BAC, submit the lacking documents for the evaluation of the Audit Team without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspension, if warranted by evidence.

7.5.	We recommended and Management agreed that the –
Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (MDRRMO) to –
submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the Audit Team on or before the 15th day after the end of each month for verification and review; and
monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF based on the programs, projects and activities listed in the LDRRMIP to ensure the proper implementation of the planned activities;
Municipal General Services Office prepare and submit the mandatory reports for the donated relief goods and donated PPE in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 25, 2014; and
Management comply with Items 2.2.6 to 2.2.8 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 and DILG MC No. 2010-101 dated September 23, 2010.

8.11.	We recommended and Management agreed that the –
Management require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to fully comply with its mandated functions as enumerated in R.A. No. 9003 and its IRR; and
Local Chief Executive, through the MSWMB –
revisit and modify priorities, if applicable, as the plan is being implemented; and
pursue its plan, as contained in its MSWMP, to establish a long-term institutionalized process aimed at having law abiding citizens through the implementation of laws, rules and regulation and develop on enormous number of people who have the love and care for the environment.

9.11.	We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Municipal Accounting Office–
perform a review on all transactions relating to business taxes which led to the erroneous calculated tax due resulting to under collection totaling ₱194,825.70 and submit the result in writing to the Audit Team for further evaluation and audit decision, without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of Charge, if warranted by evidence;
revisit the tax assessment and tax collection made on the provided data that lacks sufficient and appropriate details and submit the result in writing to the Audit Team; and
effect appropriate accounting  treatment  on the  overcollections  of ₱631,904.52 based on existing  standards.

10.7.	With the above observations, we recommended and Management agreed to –
continuously comply with the provisions of Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) –Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG)-Department of Budget and Management (DBM)- National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 as guidance in the localization of Magna Carta of Women (MCW) specifically in the preparation of Gender and Development (GAD) Plan and Budget; and
submit to the Audit Team the action plan in addressing the delays and non-implementation of the planned PPAs.
15.3.	We recommended that Management continue its compliance to existing laws, rules and regulations in order to continuously avoid audit suspensions, disallowances and charges.

1.8	We recommended and Management agreed to be guided under the GPPB Circular No. 02-2021 in preparing the project requirements during the implementation of COVID-19 Vaccination program and instruct the Bids and Award Committee to be kept abreast of the new rules, regulations and guidelines pertinent to the more effective discharge of their duties   that may be issued by authorities from time to time, and to ensure full compliance thereto.

2.13		We recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant of the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible from them as required by Section 20 of the Manual of NGAS, Volume 1;
The Municipal Accountant maintain SLs based on the certified list of taxpayers provided by the Municipal Treasurer so as to support the reported RPT/SET Receivable control accounts; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant update both their records of any changes in the list of taxpayers to reflect realistic RPT/SET receivables and collections.

3.16	We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that –
Municipal Accountant (i) adhere to perpetual method of inventory recording to avoid inconsistencies thereof; and (ii) assign a staff who will maintain the SLC containing the details for each receipt, issuances and remaining balances of all types of supplies; and
General Service Officer (i) maintain stock cards to properly record the items and identify the remaining balances; and (ii) prepare and submit the SSMI on a weekly basis to the Accounting Officer for proper accounting thereof.

4.14		We recommended the following:
The Inventory Committee to (i) conduct physical inventory of all PPEs as prescribed with Section 124 of Chapter 7 of the LGU – NGAS Manual witnessed by the Auditor or authorized representative; and (ii) prepare and duly submit RPCPPE to the offices mentioned under the instructions stated in the same manual;
The Municipal Accounting Office and the GSO to (i) maintain updated PLC and PC, respectively; (ii) reconcile the PPE SL to property cards maintained by the General Services Office as it is deemed necessary in supporting the accuracy of presented PPE balances in the financial statement; and (iii) designate in the Accounting Office and GSO persons knowledgeable in Microsoft Excel who will maintain and monitor the movement of PPE items; and
The Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments for (i)  non-PPE items and reclassify them to proper accounts; and (ii) missing and unserviceable items as identified during the inventory taking, based on proper supporting documents.

5.24		We recommended that the –
MPDO, MEO and BAC -
conduct a thorough planning for the implementation of development projects by clearly identifying the project procurement timelines as laid down in the Project Procurement Management Plan and Annual Procurement Plan or their equivalent;
consider the appropriate timing and phasing of the development projects’ implementation taking into account the constraints brought about by the still on-going health crisis and set out clearly-defined roles and responsibilities in project planning, management and monitoring of the offices concerned in accordance with their mandate set forth in RA No. 7160;
supervise and monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under the 20% DF so that the necessary and timely intervention can be made to ensure their completion in a timely fashion; and
submit all the lacking documents enumerated above.
Municipal Budget Officer ensure compliance with guidelines for changes in the annual budget that may be done through supplemental budgets as provided in 2.6a of the Budget Operations Manual for Local Government Units.

6.12		We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the concerned officials to submit the mentioned required documents to support the payment of the listed expenditures and to fully establish the validity of the claims pertaining to the disbursements charged under 5% DRRM Fund.

7.6		We recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to instruct the accountable officers/collectors of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to:
Avoid erasures and alterations in the ORs. Diligence in the presentation and recording of accounts should at all times be observed;
Submit the duplicate copy of the four CTCs; and
Observe the existing rules and regulations on the proper use, reporting and safekeeping of all accountable forms in their custody.
12.3		We recommended and Management agreed to continue its compliance to existing laws, rules and regulations in order to continuously avoid audit suspensions, disallowances and charges.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the concerned officials to –

a.) maintain supplies ledger cards and stock cards, containing the details for each receipts, issuances and remaining balances of all supplies;

b.) use moving average method as basis for the costing of supplies; and

c.) submit the summary of supplies and materials issued regularly to the Accounting Office for proper accounting thereof.
observations.managementAction: GSO & Accounting assigns additional personnel to do necessary supplies monitoring ledger, stock card and other necessary records & reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to coordinate with the Authorized Government Bank officials and employees for the processing of the same.
observations.managementAction: Payroll system using automated teller machine is now in process for full implementation

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the concerned offices
to (i) submit the required documents and/or written justification to prove the validity of the payments; (ii) revert the unutilized amount of ₱29,373.00 to the National Treasury; and (iii) ensure the correctness of the details being posted on government website to avoid public’s confusion.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Human Resource
Officer to submit (i) Daily Time Record (DTR) or any proof of services rendered of
its employees and officials duly certified by the Head of Office; (ii) accomplishment
report; and (iii) copy of an Order issued by the Head of Agency/ Office authorizing
the employees to physically report for work at their respective offices or work station
during the period of implementation of Enhanced Community Quarantine.

For the Special Risk Allowance, we recommended and Management agreed to
instruct the concerned officials to submit supporting documents that corresponds
with the period of Enhanced Community Quarantine to avoid misrepresentation on
the payment made.

We recommended and Management agreed to –
a) submit the justifications/required documents on all the deficiencies noted,
such as inconsistencies/discrepancies on the number of attendees on actual
attendance and information on various documents;
b) prepare an activity budget and/ or project proposal to set as guidelines in
the future implementation of their planned programs; and
c) ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation
required under COA Circular 2012-001 and other relevant rules and
regulations to establish the validity, propriety and regularity of the
transactions.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the concerned officials
to submit the mentioned required documents to support the payment of the listed
expenditures to ensure that the disbursements charged under 5% DRRM Fund was
sufficient to establish the validity of the claim.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and management agreed that the  
Accounting and Treasury Offices reconcile the records of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible with the annual assessment roll prepared by the Municipal Assessor to attain the intended check and balance.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Assessor provided the 2019 Assessment Roll, Municipal Treasurer provided the Certified List of taxpayers with the amounts due & collectible for the year and the Municipal Accountant is in process of recording at Subsidiary Ledger.

(ii) establish and maintain fully the GAD Database.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that:

a) revisit the MOA for the disposal of solid waste and propose amendment/s to effectively manage waste collection contract specifically on schedule of collections and basis of billings. In relation to the collection schedule, study the volume of wastes generated and the frequency of collection to be made so as to properly schedule the collection date with special consideration to the fullness of the bin.

b) Consider the option of reducing or altering collection charge to be based on weight of bins instead of the full measurement of the bin whether full or not.

c) Add provisions for liquidated damages to protect the Municipality in case of Contractor’s failure or delay of services.

d) Management should consider the study of the data on types of wastes and recyclables generated; types of recyclables not collected; how much (rough estimate) waste and recyclables the municipality typically generates and discuss alternative options with the Metro Clark with regards to the recyclable materials included in the solid wastes being collected. The value of recyclable materials may be deducted from the billing or Management may consider other means to re-use the materials.
observations.managementAction: Study of waste types/ classification is being done and MOA with Metro Clark Waste Management Corporation is under review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the requirements of Section 100 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2009-006 on the timely and complete submission of the reports for audit.
observations.managementAction: Timely submission of Disbursement Vouchers is being implemented by Municipal Accountant. Municipal Treasurer render monthly reports and accounts within the precribed time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant will be guided by Section 79 of PD 1445 with regards to immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties PPE and de-recognition thereof to present the accounts fairly.
observations.managementAction: The GSO sent a letter of invitation to COA to witness the inventory/physical count dated March 2, 2018. And the continuation of physical counting of SEF was done last Oct. 15-26, 2018. The GSO and Management further certify that the Physical Inventory Report of SEF as of March 31, 2019 has been submitted and was received by COA last May 8, 2019. PPEs under General Fund as of June 30, 2019 were already classified as good, unserviceable, broken, donated, totally damaged, and beyond repair and was submitted to COA dated July 11, 2019. The GSO is on process of property tagging on Gen. Fund and SEF (in all schools of Bagac).

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and management agreed that the (a) Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant the certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible for the year to serve as basis for setting up RPT/SET receivables and maintenance of subsidiary ledger in the Accounting Office in compliance with Section 20, Volume 1 of NGAS Manual for LGUs; and (b) Accounting and Treasury Offices reconcile the records of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible with the annual assessment roll prepared by the Municipal Assessor to attain the intended check and balance.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the concerned officials to (a) prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) as basis for the proper disposal thereof; and (b) effect the eventual derecognition/dropping from the books of the disposed properties for proper presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that the GFPS/Committee on GAD to (i) require the Accounting Unit to immediately submit the GAD Utilization Report which tally with the amount reported in the GAD AR; (ii) adhere strictly to the provisions of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC 2013-01 to ensure proper implementation of GAD PPAs; and (iii) establish and maintain fully the GAD Database.

We recommended and Management agreed to (i) instruct the implementing offices to monitor the status of project implementation regularly so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time for optimum attainment of the intent of the 20% Development Fund; and (ii) exercise prudence to ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to be guided by R.A. 10121 and JMC No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013 in planning, appropriating and implementing PPAs to ensure the efficient utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund in order that the objectives of the various programs, projects and activities funded therefrom are achieved.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
revisit the MOA for the disposal of solid waste and propose amendment/s to effectively manage waste collection contract specifically on schedule of collections and basis of billings. In relation to the collection schedule, study the volume of wastes generated and the frequency of collection to be made so as to properly schedule the collection date with special consideration to the fullness of the bin.
Consider the option of reducing or altering collection charge to be based on weight of bins instead of the full measurement of the bin whether full or not.
Add provisions for liquidated damages to protect the Municipality in case of Contractor’s failure or delay of services.
Management should consider the study of the data on types of wastes and recyclables generated; types of recyclables not collected; how much (rough estimate) waste and recyclables the municipality typically generates and discuss alternative options with the Metro Clark with regards to the recyclable materials included in the solid wastes being collected. The value of recyclable materials may be deducted from the billing or Management may consider other means to re-use the materials.

We recommended and Management agreed to consider the option of adopting the automation of payroll system for the payment of salaries/wages of permanent and JO personnel and coordinate with the Authorized Government Bank officials and employees for the processing of the same.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the (a) Municipal Treasurer to render the monthly reports and accounts within the prescribed period; and (b) Municipal Accountant to comply with the requirements of Section 100 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 2009-006 on the timely and complete submission of the reports for audit.
We recommended and Management agreed to continue its compliance to existing laws, rules and regulations in order to avoid audit suspensions, disallowances and charges.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant the certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible for the year to serve as basis for setting up RPT/SET Receivables and maintenance of subsidiary ledger in the Accounting Office in compliance with Section 20, Volume 1 of NGAS Manual. We also recommended that the Accounting and Treasury Offices reconcile the records of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible with the annual assessment roll prepared by the Municipal Assessor to attain the intended check and balance.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Treasurer updated the certified list of balances (receivables to date) and certified list of collectibles for the year to set-up the receivables (detailed with names and amount).
The management continues to comply with the standard procedures in documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management monitor and evaluate the implementation of the development projects to ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipal Government.
observations.managementAction: The management ensures that the 20% CDF will be utilized to promote social and economic development.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management be guided by JMC No. 2013-01 in planning, appropriating and implementing PPAs to ensure the efficient utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) in order that the objectives of the various programs, projects and activities funded therefrom are achieved.
observations.managementAction: Some of the expenses in some activities were shouldered by Provincial Disaster Risk Reduction Office. The hazard mapping also implemented and was shouldered by LGU. The quick response documentation (relief goods) was not properly done and accounted because all the staff in DRRM office was assigned as support in rescue operations and concentrated to critical areas affected by typhoon “Josie”.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement and revenue vouchers, financial reports, and other schedules to the Auditor within the period prescribed by existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Department has a cut-off period that leads to sufficient time in preparing financial reports.
observations.reason: The Management exerted efforts in submitting the necessary financial reports and documents, however, some of the contracts were submitted beyond the deadline.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant the certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to serve as basis for setting up RPT/SET Receivables and maintenance of subsidiary ledger in the Accounting Office. We also recommended that the Accounting and Treasury Offices reconcile the records of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible with the annual assessment roll prepared by the Municipal Assessor to attain the intended check and balance under Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, thus present an accurate RPT/SET Receivables.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council monitor the full implementation of the LDRRM plan towards the achievement of the objectives of RA No. 10121. We also recommend that the Accounting Department effect the necessary correcting entry for the purchased tools and equipment.

We recommended and Management agreed to (a) develop and implement more GAD related programs/activities that advocate women empowerment, promote gender equality and improve the welfare of women that will protect and fulfill their rights; (b) institute an effective measure to monitor and ensure that implemented-GAD-related projects are relevant and operational; and (c) fully utilize the GAD funds for GAD-related activities to achieve potentials of men and women that will contribute to local development.

We recommended that Management monitor and evaluate the implementation of the development projects to ensure that the 20% Development Fund is optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipal Government.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant submit within seven (7) days after release of check, the cash advance voucher for confidential expenses with covering transmittal letter enumerating the supporting documents in a sealed envelope, to the COA Audit Team Leader for post audit.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 10 (The cash advance for confidential fund for 4th quarter of CY 2018 was not submitted for audit within seven days after the release of check.)

observations.recommendation: 1.7	We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant the certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible for the year to serve as basis for setting up RPT/SET Receivables and maintenance of subsidiary ledger in the Accounting Office in compliance with Section 20, Volume 1 of NGAS Manual. We also recommended that the Accounting and Treasury Offices reconcile the records of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible with the annual assessment roll prepared by the Municipal Assessor to attain the intended check and balance.

4.15	We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant will be guided by Section 79 of PD 1445 with regards to immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties PPE and de-recognition thereof to present the accounts fairly.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct (a) the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of the PPEs with the presence of COA representative as witness so as to know the actual existence and condition of PPE; (b) Accounting Office effect the necessary adjusting entries to present the PPE fairly; and (c) the Assessor’s Office and Treasurer’s Office to facilitate the transfer to the Municipality of Bagac of the Land with Property Identification Number 018-03-002-02-023 still under the name of the Roman Catholic Church to protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: Management conducted a physical inventory of PPEs under General Fund but did not conduct an inventory for the PPEs under the Special Education Fund. Included in Observation No. 4

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile the records of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible with the annual assessment roll prepared by the Municipal Assessor to attain the intended check and balance under Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:                                                    (a) strengthen the Gad Focal Point Technical Working Group;                                                                            (b) conduct gender audit and institutionalized GAD database/ sex- disaggregated dta which shall be the bases of GAD planning and budgeting; and                                                                                                               (c) fully utilize the GAD funds for GAD-related activities to achieve potentials of men and women that will contribute to local development.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant submit within seven (7) days after release of check, the cash advance voucher for confidential expenses with covering transmittal letter enumerating the supporting documents in a sealed envelope, to the COA Audit Team Leader for post audit.
observations.reason: Reiterated in an audit observation memorandum. (The cash advance for confidential fund for 1st quarter of CY 2018 was submitted for audit within seven days after the release of check.)

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant the certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to serve as basis for setting up RPT/SET Receivables and maintenance of subsidiary ledger in the Accounting Office. We also recommended that the Accounting and Treasury Offices reconcile the records of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible with the annual assessment roll prepared by the Municipal Assessor to attain the intended check and balance under Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, thus present an accurate RPT/SET Receivables.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct (a) the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of the PPEs with the presence of COA representative as witness so as to know the actual existence and condition of PPE; (b) Accounting Office effect the necessary adjusting entries to present the PPE fairly; and (c) the Assessor’s Office and Treasurer’s Office to facilitate the transfer to the Municipality of Bagac of the Land with Property Identification Number 018-03-002-02-023 still under the name of the Roman Catholic Church to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended and Management agreed to:     
(a) assign personnel to the General Services Office and create an inventory team to conduct annual physical inventory of all PPE; and 


(b) instruct the General Services Office to maintain updated property cards to reconcile with the subsidiary ledger cards maintained in the accounting department as required under Sections 114, 120, 123 and 124 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I.
observations.reason:
Management created an Inventory Team but did not conduct the annual physical inventory. Reiterated in Observation no. 2

Partially Implemented

General Services Office prepared property cards but reconciliation is ongoing with accounting department.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the: 

(a) Treasury Department assign personnel to handle the custodianship of the PPE and maintain/ update/ reconcile the Property Cards with the PPELC of the Accounting Department; 

(b) Inventory Committee properly label the equipment for easy identification; and 

(c) Municipal Administrator coordinate with the Management of the Municipality of Orion for the proper disposition of the borrowed motor vehicle.
observations.managementAction: The management is now coordinating with the Municipality of Orion for the rightful disposal of the borrowed vehicle, the process is now ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality submit the GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG Regional Office within the prescribed period stated in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013.
observations.reason: No GAD Focal Person for FY 2015 and lack of expertise in doing the Gender-Responsiveness Planning and Budgeting resulted to failure to submit the GAD Plan FY 2016 Reiterated in Observation No. 4

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile the records of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible with the annual assessment roll prepared by the Municipal Assessor to attain the intended check and balance under Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS.

We recommended and Management agreed to (a) assign personnel to the General Services Office and create an inventory team to conduct annual physical inventory of all PPE; and (b) instruct the General Services Officer to maintain updated property cards to reconcile with the subsidiary ledger cards maintained in the accounting department as required under Sections 114, 120, 123 and 124 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) strengthen the GAD Focal Point Technical Working Group; (b) conduct gender audit and institutionalize GAD database/sex-disaggregated data which shall be the bases of GAD planning and budgeting; and (c) fully utilize the GAD funds for GAD-related activities to achieve potentials of men and women that will contribute to local development.

We recommended and Management agreed to prudently plan, monitor, evaluate and prioritize the implementation of projects to maximize the utilization of the 20% development fund on programs/projects/activities outlined under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 and achieve the desired benefits in socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant submit within seven (7) days after release of check, the cash advance voucher for confidential expenses with covering transmittal letter enumerating the supporting documents in a sealed envelope, to the COA Audit Team Leader for post audit.
We recommended and Management agreed to continue its compliance to existing laws, rules and regulations in order to avoid audit suspensions, disallowances and charges.

We recommended that Management follow-up the application for special land use permit at the DENR to be able to construct their Materials Recovery Facility and make representation requesting schedule of presentation of their 10- year Solid Waste Management Plan to be finalized and approved.
observations.managementAction: The application was already submitted and still on process.
observations.reason: Waiting for DENR’s instruction on the schedule of presentation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:

(a) exert effort to intensify collections and generate strategies to collect all delinquencies;





(b) oblige all stallholders to update and pay all rentals due before renewing their contract; and





(c) amend and include  provisions on the Contract of Lease that will clearly specify the payment of advance monthly rental, mode of payment, penalties and legal actions to be imposed concerning violations of the provisions.
observations.managementAction:
Novel strategies regarding letter collection to all delinquent stallholders being prepared for implementation.
Amendments and modification for contract of lease. 

Preparation is ongoing regarding provision of advance payment in rental and mode of payment and imposition of legal actions in case of violation. Implementation shall be strictly observed.
observations.reason: Absence of legal officer who will facilitate the preparation and implementation of the recommendation
